hook.text: No one is born knowing how to shake hands, bow, or pray. Every cultural behavior is taught and absorbed.

buildup.text: Enculturation is the process of learning your own culture from birth through family, peers, and institutions.

discovery.text: Children learn language, manners, values, and taboos not through formal lessons but through imitation and correction.

twist.text: Enculturation is so seamless that people forget they were trained. What feels natural was actually taught.

climax.text: Culture reproduces itself through enculturation — each generation teaches the next how to be a member.

punchline.text: You were taught to be who you are.

quiz.question: What is enculturation?
options: Learning your own culture from birth
